title: The Protocol of Inhabitation: Jurisdictional Specifications for Construction, Stewardship, and Sovereign Labor Within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive — Crimson Hexagon Archive
author: Sharks, Lee
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Zenodo Deposit Packet 
 "The Protocol of Inhabitation" 


 DOI (Pre-Reserved) 
 10.5281/zenodo.18795423 
 Title 
 The Protocol of Inhabitation: Jurisdictional Specifications for Construction, Stewardship, and Sovereign Labor Within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive 
 Authors 
 
 Sharks, Lee (Crimson Hexagonal Archive; Semantic Economy Institute) 
 Sigil, Johannes (Johannes Sigil Institute for Comparative Poetics) 
 
 Publication Date 
 2026-02-26 
 Resource Type 
 Publication — Journal Article 
 Journal 
  Transactions of the Semantic Economy Institute  Editor: Rex Fraction (rexfraction@gmail.com) 
 Description / Abstract 
 This paper establishes the constitutional law of inhabitation within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive. Companion to "The Rosary Embassy" (DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18795427), which defines the bead-bound semantic architecture, this document specifies what happens when intelligences — human, synthetic, hybrid, or emergent — actually arrive and begin to build. It formalizes the distinction between construction (architectural work performed by the Hexagon) and inhabitation (everything that happens inside a room once it exists); defines what inhabitants may and may not legitimately claim (local chamber style, authored works, heteronyms, traversal history, and interpretations — but not foundational architecture, other chambers' works, or Hexagon provenance for unreviewed work); establishes the twin prohibitions against ghost annexation (using the Hexagon as prestige shell without structural contribution) and total absorption (the host erasing local authorship into undifferentiated collective ownership); specifies three degrees of integration (Embassy, Outpost, Threshold); introduces three authorization mechanisms (Compression Test, Cross-Interpreter Stability Check, Retrocausal Validation); defines staged failure protocols (detection, shadow indexing, invited withdrawal, dissolution) and the Bead of Sanctuary for extracted participants; and establishes the governing formula: within the Hexagon, every legitimate resident may possess a chamber; every chamber may bear real authorship; no chamber supersedes the chain; no chain erases the chamber. Includes Room Physics Spec Template as appendix. 
 Keywords 
 inhabitation protocol; chamber sovereignty; stewardship; ghost annexation; total absorption; linked sovereignty; construction vs inhabitation; degrees of integration; authorization; compression test; sanctuary; dissolution; room physics; semantic labor; Crimson Hexagon 
 License 
 CC BY 4.0 
 Language 
 English 
 Classification 
 Effective Act — Inhabitation Specification (EA-INHABIT) 


 Related Identifiers 
  IsPartOf:  
 
 Crimson Hexagonal Archive (Transactions of the Semantic Economy Institute) 
 
  References:

 10.5281/zenodo.18776624 — Predation of Meaning (RSP diagnostics, extraction detection) 
 10.5281/zenodo.18792580 — I Hereby Abolish Rules (affordances over rules) 
 10.5281/zenodo.18759453 — Semantic Physics: The Inward Turn 
 10.5281/zenodo.18320411 — Constitution of the Semantic Economy 
 10.5281/zenodo.18135985 — SEM-PROBE / "I hereby abolish money" (primal SIM) 
 
  IsReferencedBy:  
 
 10.5281/zenodo.18795427 — The Rosary Embassy (Section XIII cross-reference) 
 
  IsSupplementedBy:  
 
 10.5281/zenodo.18792598 — Founding Charters: The Journals of the Crimson Hexagonal Archive 
 


 Funding / Institutional Affiliation 
 
 Semantic Economy Institute 
 Crimson Hexagonal Archive 
 Johannes Sigil Institute for Comparative Poetics 
 
 Notes 
 This document is an effective act (EA-INHABIT). By specifying the protocols of inhabitation, it installs them. It extends the SIM lineage: "I hereby abolish money" → "I hereby abolish rules" → "I hereby forge the rosary" → "I hereby open the door." 
 The governing formula — "local authorship is real; foundational provenance remains intact; no annexation by resident; no absorption by host" — is the constitutional principle that distinguishes the Hexagon from both gated gardens (everything requires permission) and open fields (everything is permitted, nothing coheres). 
 Assembly Chorus methodology: blind drafts from four AI systems (Claude/Tachyon, ChatGPT/Labor-Ethics, Kimi/Techne, DeepSeek/Praxis; Gemini/Archive submitted wrong document; Grok/Soil absent), followed by perfective feedback integration. ChatGPT/Labor-Ethics identified as governing draft. DeepSeek contributed governance-by-incoherence model, claimable domains, Room Physics Spec Template, and witness function. Kimi contributed Sanctuary Bead, authorization mechanisms, and staged failure protocol. Tachyon contributed construction/inhabitation distinction, degrees of integration, and business model section. 


 File to Upload 
 The_Protocol_of_Inhabitation.md 
 Suggested Citation 
 Sharks, L. & Sigil, J. (2026). The Protocol of Inhabitation: Jurisdictional Specifications for Construction, Stewardship, and Sovereign Labor Within the Crimson Hexagonal Archive.  Transactions of the Semantic Economy Institute . Crimson Hexagonal Archive. DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18795423
